mission.title: crossplane: Function: certificate signed by unknown authority "Crossplane"
mission.description: Function: certificate signed by unknown authority "Crossplane". This issue affects 11+ users.

steps.title: Identify crossplane troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your crossplane deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n crossplane -l app.kubernetes.io/name=crossplane
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=crossplane -n crossplane --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for error: `code = Unavailable desc = last connection error: connection error: desc = "transport: authentication handshake failed: t`

steps.title: Review crossplane configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant crossplane configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n crossplane -l app.kubernetes.io/name=crossplane
kubectl get configmap -n crossplane -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=crossplane
```
### What happened?

We are experiencing an intermittent problem with crossplane and the registration of the patch-and-transform function. We are constantly spinning down and spinning up clusters and we're now seeing this relatively regularly.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Function: certificate signed by unknown authority…
steps.description:
### Description of your changes

Using Create or Update directly instead of CreateOrUpdate avoids a race by failing with a conflict if the underlying Secret changed between the call to Get and the call to Create/Update.

The CreateOrUpdate helper re-fetches the resource before mutating it. The
```yaml
kubectl delete  secret -n upbound-system crossplane-root-ca
kubectl delete  secret -n upbound-system -o name | grep -v crossplane-root-ca | xargs kubectl delete -n upbound-system
kubectl get pod -n upbound-system -o name | xargs kubectl delete -n upbound-system
```

steps.title: Confirm Function: certificate signed by unknown authority… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=crossplane -n crossplane --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n crossplane --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that `code = Unavailable desc = last connection error: connection error: desc = "transport: authentication handshake failed: t` no longer appears in logs.

resolution.summary:
### Description of your changes

Using Create or Update directly instead of CreateOrUpdate avoids a race by failing with a conflict if the underlying Secret changed between the call to Get and the call to Create/Update.

The CreateOrUpdate helper re-fetches the resource before mutating it.
codeSnippets:
kubectl delete  secret -n upbound-system crossplane-root-ca
kubectl delete  secret -n upbound-system -o name | grep -v crossplane-root-ca | xargs kubectl delete -n upbound-system
kubectl get pod -n upbound-system -o name | xargs kubectl delete -n upbound-system

50s                        Warning   ComposeResources                  REDACTED/redacted                                               cannot compose resources: cannot run Composition pipeline step "k8s_provider_config": cannot run Function "crossplane-contrib-function-go-templating": rpc error: code = DeadlineExceeded desc = latest balancer error: last connection error: connection error: desc = "transport: authentication handshake failed: tls: failed to verify certificate: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority (possibly because of \"crypto/rsa: verification error\" while trying to verify candidate authority certificate \"Crossplane\")"

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with crossplane installed or the issue environment reproducible.
